results.possibleIdentity: M. J. Becker was an engineer and scientist associated with Kimberly-Clark Corporation during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was specifically part of the company's Peter J. Schweitzer Division, which was the world's leading manufacturer of condenser paper and other thin dielectric materials used in the production of condensers (capacitors). Becker was based in New York City, where the Peter J. Schweitzer Division maintained its headquarters at 261 Madison Avenue. He is known to have participated in the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) program, a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. Becker's information was recorded in the E.A.T. Technical Services Index, which matched artists with engineers based on their technical expertise; his index card identified his specialization in condensers and condenser materials.
